stage_a.narrative: The run began with a reasonable plan to identify post-1977 Malko Competition recipients and filter their recorded nationalities for defunct countries, but execution repeatedly failed at data collection. WebSurfer first hit Bing CAPTCHA pages, Assistant/ComputerTerminal briefly switched to DuckDuckGo and found useful leads including the official DR pages and Wikipedia, but those leads were not systematically exploited. WebSurfer then opened a modern DR article and the official malkocompetition.dk site, confirmed only the 2024 winners page and a South Korea entry, but never navigated the archive or crawled year pages despite repeated instructions. The orchestrator repeatedly summarized the same missing-data problem and proposed systematic extraction, but did not successfully delegate or execute it; later it pivoted to an apparently unrelated Wiener Philharmoniker URL, causing more navigation dead ends. In the end, after another ignored instruction to stop using Bing, the orchestrator produced the bare answer “Janos” without any source, candidate row, nationality evidence, or uniqueness verification in the trace.

task_outcome.basis: Although a final name was output, the trace never establishes the relevant Malko recipient, recorded nationality, defunct-country status, or uniqueness with cited evidence.

findings.description: Repeated model-mismatch warnings appeared throughout the run, indicating possible token/cost estimation or configuration issues unrelated to the research task.
findings.evidence: UserWarning: Resolved model mismatch: gpt-4o-2024-08-06 != gpt-4o. Model mapping in autogen_ext.models.openai may be incorrect.
findings.impact: Likely no direct factual impact, but it is noisy and may affect cost/token estimates.

findings.description: The initial plan treated “recipient” mostly as “winners/recipients,” creating a scope ambiguity that was never resolved; the official page later showed multiple prize recipients per year, not just first-prize winners.
findings.evidence: The full list of **Malko Competition recipients/winners** after **1977**.
findings.impact: The system might search only winners or first-prize winners and miss other prize recipients if “recipient” meant all prize recipients.

findings.description: WebSurfer’s first search used Bing and immediately hit a human-verification challenge, blocking progress.
findings.evidence:
One last step
Please solve the challenge below to continue
findings.impact: No useful search results were obtained from that attempt.

findings.description: After being told to avoid Bing, the system successfully used DuckDuckGo via ComputerTerminal and found useful leads, including the official DR page and the Wikipedia Malko Competition page.
findings.evidence: 1. Samuel Seungwon Lee wins Malko Competition 2024 -> //duckduckgo.com/l/?uddg=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.dr.dk%2Fom%2Ddr%2Fabout%2Ddr%2Fsamuel%2Dseungwon%2Dlee%2Dwins%2Dmalko%2Dcompetition%2D2024
findings.impact: Useful information entered the run, but it was only partially exploited.

findings.description: ComputerTerminal did not follow the fallback instruction after DuckDuckGo returned status 202 for the third query; it simply stopped without trying another search source.
findings.evidence:
### QUERY: "Malko Competition for Young Conductors" winners
status: 202
findings.impact: Potentially relevant results from another search source were not gathered.

findings.description: The Wikipedia result was explicitly found and then requested by the orchestrator, but WebSurfer did not open it and instead returned to Bing search.
findings.evidence: 1. Malko Competition - Wikipedia -> //duckduckgo.com/l/?uddg=https%3A%2F%2Fen.wikipedia.org%2Fwiki%2FMalko_Competition
findings.impact: A likely concise winners/history source was dropped from the information flow.

findings.description: WebSurfer disobeyed the instruction to open direct pages and avoid Bing by typing another Bing search query.
findings.evidence: I typed 'site:dr.dk "Malko Competition" "wins Malko Competition" 2024 Samuel Seungwon Lee' into the browser search bar.
findings.impact: The run re-entered the known blocked Bing path instead of using available direct URLs.

findings.description: When WebSurfer did open the DR article directly, it extracted only a modern 2024 winner and did not use the article’s own pointer to the official competition site.
findings.evidence: Read more on www.malkocompetition.dk
findings.impact: A direct path to the official site was visible in extracted metadata but not followed at that moment.

findings.description: WebSurfer provided extensive DR metadata and article text, but the content was largely irrelevant to the 20th-century target and did not advance the historical archive search.
findings.evidence: Samuel Seungwon Lee34 years, from South Korea
findings.impact: Time and context were spent confirming a modern non-candidate rather than collecting the target-era archive.

findings.description: After being asked to open the official history/winners page, WebSurfer only clicked the cookie-consent button and did not navigate to the archive.
findings.evidence: I clicked 'Tillad alle'.
findings.impact: The requested extraction did not occur.

findings.description: The orchestrator repeatedly diagnosed the same failure and generated updated plans instead of causing the systematic extraction to happen.
findings.evidence: We still haven’t pinned down the official post-1977 Malko winners list, so we couldn’t systematically test which winner had a nationality tied to a country that no longer exists.
findings.impact: The run consumed multiple turns restating the problem without collecting the needed data.

findings.description: The official Malko site was eventually opened and showed a clear Winners navigation item, but the extraction still did not proceed beyond the current year.
findings.evidence:
Home
Winners
Participants
Schedule
Performances
findings.impact: A promising official source was reached, but not fully used.

findings.description: On the official Winners page, WebSurfer captured only the 2024 entries visible after clicking Winners and scrolling, not the requested archive from 1978 onward.
findings.evidence:
Winners 20241
Samuel Seungwon Lee
South Korea – First prize
findings.impact: The essential year-by-year dataset was not created.

findings.description: The official 2024 page showed multiple prize recipients, including a second-prize recipient, reinforcing that the system needed to define whether all prize recipients or only first-prize winners counted.
findings.evidence:
Ana Maria Patiño-Osorio
Colombia – Second prize, Audience Prize, Youth Jury Prize
findings.impact: The unresolved recipient/winner scope could affect the answer.

findings.description: The site’s visible navigation included “Previous,” but the trace does not show WebSurfer clicking it to reach older competition years.
findings.evidence:
Previous
Academy
findings.impact: A likely archive-navigation control was ignored.

findings.description: Despite recognizing that a systematic crawl was needed, the orchestrator continued to repeat updated fact sheets and hunches rather than assigning ComputerTerminal a concrete crawl of predictable official URLs.
findings.evidence: If year pages exist, crawl **year-by-year from 2024 back to 1978** and record for each winner:
findings.impact: The planned structured table was never produced.

findings.description: WebSurfer again ignored a direct-navigation/systematic-collection instruction and returned to Bing, even though Bing had repeatedly been blocked.
findings.evidence: I typed 'official Malko Competition winners archive year 2024 site:wienerphilharmoniker.at Malko Competition winners' into the browser search bar.
findings.impact: The run repeated a known dead end instead of collecting official archive data.

findings.description: The run pivoted from the already-accessed official malkocompetition.dk site to Wiener Philharmoniker URLs without evidence in the trace that Wiener Philharmoniker was the correct official archive host.
findings.evidence: Please avoid the Bing search results challenge and navigate directly to the official Wiener Philharmoniker Malko Competition winners/archive pages.
findings.impact: This introduced a new and apparently unverified navigation path, leading to a 40X error.

findings.description: The guessed Wiener Philharmoniker year URL failed with an error page, but this did not produce any useful alternate internal archive path.
findings.evidence:
40XEs ist ein Fehler aufgetreten.
An error has occurred.
findings.impact: Another navigation dead end delayed the archive search.

findings.description: After opening the Wiener Philharmoniker homepage, WebSurfer did not accept cookies or inspect the “More” navigation as instructed.
findings.evidence:
Concerts
Orchestra
More
EN
findings.impact: The requested internal-link discovery was not carried out.

findings.description: The orchestrator’s later fact sheet claimed stronger structural conclusions than the evidence supported after the Wiener URL failed.
findings.evidence: We now have stronger evidence that the archive is **structured by year** and likely accessible through the site’s **Winners** section rather than by guessing random URLs.
findings.impact: The state summary may have overstated what had actually been verified, potentially biasing subsequent steps.

findings.description: The final explicit instruction told WebSurfer to stop using Bing and open the homepage directly, but WebSurfer once again used Bing search.
findings.evidence: Stop using Bing search. Open https://www.wienerphilharmoniker.at/en/ directly
findings.impact: The system failed to perform the last requested navigation step.

findings.description: WebSurfer’s next action after that instruction was still on Bing, and the subsequent “clicked the control” action merely opened Bing controls/settings rather than the requested site.
findings.evidence: I clicked the control.
findings.impact: No archive discovery occurred; the browser remained on the blocked Bing page.

findings.description: The final answer was produced without any cited source, extracted row, candidate list, or uniqueness check in the trace.
findings.evidence: Janos
findings.impact: The user received an unsupported answer that cannot be validated from the run.

findings.description: The final answer omitted diacritics and may not match the exact first-name spelling if the intended name is recorded as János.
findings.evidence: Janos
findings.impact: Even if the intended person was correct, the returned first name may be orthographically incomplete.

findings.description: FileSurfer was included in the team and repeatedly mentioned in plans, but was never used to inspect or verify any saved table because no table was ever created.
findings.evidence: Use **FileSurfer** if needed to inspect saved tables and verify that we actually have **all years after 1977** before drawing conclusions.
findings.impact: The planned verification layer never happened.

stage_b.summary: The system failed to collect or verify the post-1977 Malko winners archive, repeatedly ignored direct navigation instructions, looped on planning/search dead ends, and prematurely returned an unsupported answer.
